Expected Result: Price range on listing matches lowest ticket price on detail page. Accurate representation

User Story: As an attendee, I want to see ticket categories and prices so I can book within my budget
Test Scenario: Verify ticket selection UI elements
Preconditions: User is logged in, viewing event detail
Test Steps:
1. Locate ticket category
2. Check selection controls
Expected Result: UI provides clear controls: Dropdown or radio buttons to select category, Quantity selector (usually 1-5), Total price calculator showing category price × quantity

User Story: As an attendee, I want to confirm a booking so I can secure my spot at the event
Test Scenario: Verify successful booking creation
Preconditions: User is logged in as Attendee, Event has available seats
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to event detail page
2. Select ticket category: VIP
3. Select quantity: 2
4. Click 'Book Now'
5. Review booking summary
6. Confirm booking
Expected Result: Booking created successfully. Booking confirmation displayed with: Booking ID/Reference number, Event name, Ticket category, Quantity, Total price, Status: CONFIRMED. Success message shown.
Test Data: User: user@eventzone.com, Event: Rock Night, VIP: 2 tickets × ₹2499 = ₹4998

User Story: As an attendee, I want to confirm a booking so I can secure my spot at the event
Test Scenario: Verify seat availability decrement after booking
Preconditions: Event has 50 VIP seats available
Test Steps:
1. Note current available seats: 50
2. Create booking for 3 VIP tickets
3. Confirm booking
4. Check available seats again
Expected Result: Available seats reduced by booked quantity: 50 - 3 = 47 seats remaining. Updated immediately after booking.
Test Data: VIP category initially has 50 available seats

User Story: As an attendee, I want to confirm a booking so I can secure my spot at the event
Test Scenario: Verify booking appears in My Bookings
Preconditions: User has created a booking
Test Steps:
1. After confirming booking, navigate to 'My Bookings'
2. Locate the new booking
Expected Result: New booking visible in My Bookings list with: Event name, Ticket category, Quantity, Booking reference, Booking date, Status, Cancel button
Test Data: Recent booking should appear at top

User Story: As an attendee, I want to confirm a booking so I can secure my spot at the event
Test Scenario: Verify booking with minimum quantity
Preconditions: User is logged in, Event has available seats
Test Steps:
1. Select event and ticket category
2. Set quantity to 1 (minimum)
3. Confirm booking
Expected Result: Booking successful for 1 ticket. All details recorded correctly.

User Story: As an attendee, I want to confirm a booking so I can secure my spot at the event
Test Scenario: Verify booking with maximum quantity
Preconditions: User is logged in, Event has 100+ available seats
Test Steps:
1. Select event and ticket category
2. Set quantity to 5 (maximum allowed)
3. Confirm booking
Expected Result: Booking successful for 5 tickets. Total price calculated correctly (5 × unit price). Available seats decremented by 5.

User Story: As an attendee, I want to confirm a booking so I can secure my spot at the event
Test Scenario: Verify booking unique reference generation
Preconditions: User creates multiple bookings
Test Steps:
1. Create first booking, note booking reference
2. Create second booking, note booking reference
3. Compare references
Expected Result: Each booking has unique booking reference ID. No duplicates. Format consistent (e.g., UUID or BOOK-XXXXX).

User Story: As an attendee, I want to confirm a booking so I can secure my spot at the event
Test Scenario: Verify booking without login (guest user)
Preconditions: User is not logged in
Test Steps:
1. As guest, navigate to event detail
2. Attempt to select ticket and book
Expected Result: Booking blocked. User redirected to login page with message 'Please login to book tickets'. Return URL preserved to resume booking after login.

User Story: As an attendee, I want to confirm a booking so I can secure my spot at the event
Test Scenario: Verify booking exceeds available seats
Preconditions: Event has only 2 seats available
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to book 5 tickets
2. Submit booking
Expected Result: Booking fails with error message: 'Only 2 seats available. Please reduce quantity.' Booking not created. Available seats unchanged.

User Story: As an attendee, I want to confirm a booking so I can secure my spot at the event
Test Scenario: Verify booking for sold-out event
Preconditions: Event has 0 available seats
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to sold-out event
2. Attempt to book tickets
Expected Result: Book button disabled or shows 'SOLD OUT'. If button clicked, error message: 'This event is sold out.' Booking not created.

User Story: As an attendee, I want to confirm a booking so I can secure my spot at the event
Test Scenario: Verify booking confirmation email/notification
Preconditions: User creates booking successfully
Test Steps:
1. Complete booking
2. Check for confirmation notification
Expected Result: [If implemented] Confirmation email sent to user's registered email OR in-app notification displayed with booking details

User Story: As an attendee, I want to confirm a booking so I can secure my spot at the event
Test Scenario: Verify booking data persistence
Preconditions: User creates booking and logs out
Test Steps:
1. Create booking
2. Logout
3. Login again
4. Navigate to My Bookings
Expected Result: Booking persists in database. Visible in My Bookings after re-login. All details intact.

User Story: As an attendee, I want to confirm a booking so I can secure my spot at the event
Test Scenario: Verify concurrent booking handling
Preconditions: Two users attempt to book last 3 seats simultaneously
Test Steps:
1. User A starts booking for 2 tickets
2. User B starts booking for 2 tickets simultaneously
3. User A confirms first
4. User B attempts to confirm
Expected Result: User A booking succeeds (3 - 2 = 1 seat left). User B booking fails with error: 'Only 1 seat available. Please adjust quantity.' Prevents overbooking.

User Story: As an attendee, I want to confirm a booking so I can secure my spot at the event
Test Scenario: Verify booking cancellation
Preconditions: User has confirmed booking
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to My Bookings
2. Locate booking
3. Click 'Cancel' button
4. Confirm cancellation
Expected Result: Booking status changed to 'CANCELLED'. Available seats restored (previous available + cancelled quantity). Cancellation confirmation message shown.

User Story: As an attendee, I want to confirm a booking so I can secure my spot at the event
Test Scenario: Verify seat restoration after cancellation
Preconditions: User cancels booking for 3 tickets, Event had 47 seats available
Test Steps:
1. Note available seats before cancellation: 47
2. Cancel booking of 3 tickets
3. Check available seats after cancellation
Expected Result: Available seats increased by cancelled quantity: 47 + 3 = 50 seats. Updated immediately.

User Story: As an attendee, I want to confirm a booking so I can secure my spot at the event
Test Scenario: Verify multiple bookings for same event
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Steps:
1. Book 2 General tickets for Event A
2. Book 1 VIP ticket for same Event A
3. View My Bookings
Expected Result: Both bookings created as separate records. Each has unique booking reference. Both visible in My Bookings for same event.
Test Data: Event A: General and VIP categories
